title: Smoky Charred Syrian Tofu Kebabs
description: These charred Syrian tofu kebabs are a flavorful Whole30-friendly meal prep dish that combines the smoky taste of robata-grilled tofu with a creamy and refreshing tzatziki sauce.
instructions: Cut the tofu into 1-inch cubes and place them in a shallow dish.

In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, cumin, and salt. Pour the marinade over the tofu and toss to coat. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat a robata grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated tofu, zucchini, yellow bell pepper, and red onion onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the tofu is charred and the vegetables are tender.
Meanwhile, prepare the tzatziki sauce by grating the cucumber and mixing it with the Greek yogurt, dill, and lemon zest. Season with salt to taste.
To serve, place the grilled skewers on a platter and serve with the tzatziki sauce on the side.
